introSection: Timberhawk: Ride of Prey is a wooden roller coaster located at Wild Waves Theme & Water Park in Federal Way, Washington, USA. The ride utilizes both wood and steel supports to support a wooden track. Its trains are built by Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In 2002, the city of Federal Way, Washington filed permits to build a wooden roller coaster for the 2003 season.

The roller coaster was announced in February 2003. It was the first wooden roller coaster to be built by S&S Worldwide, who went on to build three more. Timberhawk: Ride of Prey opened on June 7, 2003.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: 2 trains with 4 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 3 rows for a total of 24 riders per train.

fullText:
Timberhawk: Ride of Prey is a wooden roller coaster located at Wild Waves Theme & Water Park in Federal Way, Washington, USA. The ride utilizes both wood and steel supports to support a wooden track. Its trains are built by Philadelphia Toboggan Coasters.

In 2002, the city of Federal Way, Washington filed permits to build a wooden roller coaster for the 2003 season.

The roller coaster was announced in February 2003. It was the first wooden roller coaster to be built by S&S Worldwide, who went on to build three more. Timberhawk: Ride of Prey opened on June 7, 2003.



2 trains with 4 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in 3 rows for a total of 24 riders per train.
